name: Banned Moves and Clauses
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Banned Moves and Clauses

### Standard Clauses (Smogon)
- **Sleep Clause**: Only 1 opponent Pokemon may be put to sleep at a time
- **Species Clause**: No duplicate Pokemon on a team
- **Evasion Clause**: Double Team and Minimize are banned
- **OHKO Clause**: Fissure, Horn Drill, Guillotine are banned
- **Freeze Clause**: Only 1 Pokemon may be frozen at a time (sometimes applied)

### Notable Banned/Restricted
- No Pokemon from the Ubers list (Mewtwo, Mew, Lugia, Ho-Oh, Celebi)
- Swagger + Paralysis combo ("SwagPlay") is controversial but not officially banned in GSC
- Baton Pass is fully legal in standard GSC OU

---

name: UU (UnderUsed) Highlights
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] UU (UnderUsed) Highlights

Pokemon not commonly seen in OU but viable in their own tier:

| Pokemon | Type | Notable Traits |
|---------|------|----------------|
| Ampharos | Electric | Slow but bulky Electric; Thunder Wave + Thunderbolt |
| Gyarados | Water/Flying | High Attack but 4x Electric weakness |
| Scyther | Bug/Flying | Swords Dance sweeper; no Technician in Gen 2 |
| Electabuzz | Electric | Fast mixed attacker; Cross Chop coverage |
| Feraligatr | Water | Good Attack but outclassed by Suicune defensively |
| Slowking | Water/Psychic | Special wall; worse Slowbro in most cases |
| Kabutops | Rock/Water | Swords Dance sweeper |
| Quagsire | Water/Ground | Only weak to Grass; Earthquake + Surf |
| Granbull | Normal | High Attack; Intimidate doesn't exist yet |
| Nidoqueen | Poison/Ground | Worse Nidoking generally |
| Pinsir | Bug | Swords Dance + Submission |
| Magneton | Electric/Steel | Steel typing is defensive; decent SpA |

---

name: Meta-Game Evolution
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Meta-Game Evolution

### Early GSC Meta (late 1990s - early 2000s)
- Dominated by Snorlax + Zapdos
- Spikes was underrated initially
- Stall teams were less refined

### Modern GSC Meta (2010s - present)
- Spikes is recognized as the most important move
- Forretress + Cloyster valued highly for Spikes support
- Raikou rose to become the #2 Pokemon (best shuffler)
- Perish trap strategies became more refined
- Team building is highly optimized around Snorlax matchup
- ~20-25 truly viable OU Pokemon (small metagame by modern standards)

### Design Philosophy
GSC rewards:
- **Patience**: Games are long; chip damage from Spikes/Toxic adds up
- **Prediction**: Switching is critical; wrong switches eat Spikes + attacks
- **Team synergy**: Every member must handle specific threats
- **Risk management**: Explosion, Self-Destruct, and Belly Drum are high-risk/high-reward

name: #143 Snorlax [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #143 Snorlax [OU]

**Role:** Special Wall / Tank / CurseLax  
**Stats:** 160/110/65/30/65/110 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CurseLax: Curse / Rest / Sleep Talk / Body Slam or Double-Edge**
- **MixLax: Body Slam / Earthquake / Fire Blast or Flamethrower / Rest or Self-Destruct**
- **DrumLax: Belly Drum / Body Slam / Earthquake / Rest**

### Why It's Good
The king of GSC OU. Snorlax's enormous HP and Special Defense make it the premier special tank. CurseLax is the most dangerous late-game sweeper. It fits on virtually every team.

### Counters
Skarmory walls physical sets. Misdreavus blocks Body Slam. Machamp with Cross Chop. Tyranitar can trade blows. Roar/Whirlwind users to phaze CurseLax.

---

name: #248 Tyranitar [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #248 Tyranitar [OU]

**Role:** Mixed Attacker / Pursuit Trapper  
**Stats:** 100/134/110/61/95/100 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **MixTar: Rock Slide / Fire Blast / DynamicPunch or Earthquake / Pursuit**
- **CurseTar: Curse / Rock Slide / Earthquake / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Massive 600 BST with excellent mixed attacking stats. Pursuit traps Gengar and Starmie. DynamicPunch always confuses. Sets Sandstorm with its mere presence (not literally - no Sand Stream yet).

### Counters
Machamp, Heracross, Marowak, Steelix. 4x weak to Fighting. Water-types like Suicune and Vaporeon.

---

name: #145 Zapdos [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #145 Zapdos [OU]

**Role:** Special Attacker / RestTalker  
**Stats:** 90/90/85/100/125/90 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **RestTalk: Thunder / Hidden Power Ice / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **Offensive: Thunderbolt / Hidden Power Ice or Water / Thunder Wave / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Best Electric-type in GSC. Excellent typing with only two weaknesses. Great bulk and power. Threatens Waters and Skarmory.

### Counters
Snorlax (with high SpDef), Raikou, Blissey, Ground-types like Rhydon/Golem (immune to Thunder).

---

name: #227 Skarmory [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #227 Skarmory [OU]

**Role:** Physical Wall / Spiker / Phazer  
**Stats:** 65/80/140/70/40/70 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Spiker: Spikes / Whirlwind / Drill Peck / Rest or Toxic**
- **CursKarm: Curse / Drill Peck / Rest / Sleep Talk**

### Why It's Good
The premier physical wall. Immune to Ground, resists Normal. Spikes + Whirlwind is incredibly effective for passive damage. Walls Snorlax, Marowak, and physical attackers.

### Counters
Zapdos, Raikou (4x weak to Electric). Fire-types like Charizard. Special attackers bypass its pitiful SpDef.

---

name: #121 Starmie [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #121 Starmie [OU]

**Role:** Rapid Spinner / Special Attacker  
**Stats:** 60/75/85/115/100/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Spinner: Rapid Spin / Surf / Psychic or Thunderbolt / Recover**
- **Offensive: Surf / Thunderbolt / Psychic / Recover**

### Why It's Good
The best Rapid Spinner in GSC. Blazing speed. Good coverage with Surf/Thunderbolt/Psychic. Recover for sustainability. Vital for removing Spikes.

### Counters
Pursuit Tyranitar traps it. Snorlax tanks hits. Blissey. Dark-types immune to Psychic.

---

name: #245 Suicune [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #245 Suicune [OU]

**Role:** Bulky Water / CroSuicune  
**Stats:** 100/75/115/85/90/115 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CroSuicune: Surf / Ice Beam / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **Roar: Surf / Roar / Toxic / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Extreme physical and special bulk. RestTalk makes it very hard to kill. Roar variant is a great phazer that racks up Spikes damage.

### Counters
Zapdos, Raikou (Electric attacks). Vaporeon with Water Absorb. Snorlax can outlast. Grass-types.

---

name: #103 Exeggutor [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #103 Exeggutor [OU]

**Role:** Sleep Lead / Special Attacker / Exploder  
**Stats:** 95/95/85/55/125/65 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Sleep Powder / Psychic / Giga Drain or Hidden Power Fire / Explosion**
- **Stun Spore: Stun Spore / Sleep Powder / Psychic / Explosion**

### Why It's Good
One of the best sleep leads. Sleep Powder + Explosion makes it a potent team disruptor. Strong Psychic STAB. Can catch Steel-types with HP Fire.

### Counters
Snorlax (huge SpDef), Blissey. Heracross resists Psychic and threatens. Skarmory walls non-HP Fire sets.

---

name: #094 Gengar [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #094 Gengar [OU]

**Role:** Special Attacker / Spinblocker / Exploder  
**Stats:** 60/65/60/110/130/75 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Thunderbolt / Ice Punch / Hypnosis / Explosion**
- **Perish Trapper: Mean Look / Perish Song / Protect / Destiny Bond**

### Why It's Good
Ghost-typing blocks Rapid Spin, preserving Spikes. Levitate-like immunity to Ground (Levitate) and Normal immunity. 130 SpAtk hits hard. Hypnosis and Explosion provide disruption.

### Counters
Pursuit Tyranitar traps and KOs. Snorlax tanks special hits. Blissey. Dark-types immune to STAB.

---

name: #068 Machamp [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #068 Machamp [OU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / Snorlax Counter  
**Stats:** 90/130/80/55/65/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Cross Chop / Rock Slide / Earthquake / Hidden Power Bug or Fire Blast**
- **RestTalk: Cross Chop / Rock Slide / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **CurseChamp: Curse / Cross Chop / Rock Slide / Rest**

### Why It's Good
The premier Snorlax counter. Cross Chop 2HKOs most Snorlax. Excellent coverage. Curse sets are potent late-game.

### Counters
Skarmory walls physical attacks. Starmie outspeeds and hits with Psychic/Surf. Gengar immune to Cross Chop.

---

name: #243 Raikou [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #243 Raikou [OU]

**Role:** Special Attacker / RestTalker  
**Stats:** 90/85/75/115/115/100 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **RestTalk: Thunder / Crunch / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **Offensive: Thunderbolt / Crunch / Hidden Power Ice or Water / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Fastest relevant Electric-type. 115 SpAtk + 115 Speed is devastating. Crunch covers Psychics and Ghosts. RestTalk keeps it healthy.

### Counters
Snorlax (great SpDef), Blissey, Ground-types (immune to Electric), Rhydon, Steelix.

---

name: #105 Marowak [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #105 Marowak [OU]

**Role:** Physical Wallbreaker  
**Stats:** 60/80/110/45/50/80 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Swords Dance: Swords Dance / Earthquake / Rock Slide / Hidden Power Bug**
- **Standard: Earthquake / Rock Slide / Hidden Power Bug / Rest or Swords Dance**

### Why It's Good
With Thick Club, Marowak has the highest effective Attack in GSC. Earthquake + Rock Slide has perfect neutral coverage. Can 2HKO almost anything after Swords Dance.

### Counters
Skarmory (immune to EQ, resists Rock Slide). Exeggutor. Suicune. Starmie outspeeds. Must be careful of its poor Speed and Special bulk.

---

name: #242 Blissey [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #242 Blissey [OU]

**Role:** Special Wall  
**Stats:** 255/10/10/55/75/135 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Support: Softboiled / Heal Bell / Toxic / Light Screen or Seismic Toss**
- **Counter: Softboiled / Heal Bell / Counter / Toxic**

### Why It's Good
Literally cannot be KOed by special attacks thanks to 255 HP and 135 SpDef. Heal Bell cures team status. Counter punishes physical attackers who try to exploit 10 Defense.

### Counters
Any physical attacker: Machamp, Marowak, Snorlax with Curse. Toxic stalling.

---

name: #091 Cloyster [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #091 Cloyster [OU]

**Role:** Spiker / Physical Wall  
**Stats:** 50/95/180/70/85/45 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Spiker: Spikes / Surf / Explosion / Rapid Spin or Toxic**

### Why It's Good
180 Defense is insane. Reliable Spikes setter. Explosion is devastating. Surf for STAB. Alternative Spiker to Forretress/Skarmory.

### Counters
Special attacks (only 45 SpDef). Zapdos, Raikou. Starmie spins away Spikes.

---

name: #205 Forretress [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #205 Forretress [OU]

**Role:** Spiker / Rapid Spinner / Exploder  
**Stats:** 75/90/140/40/60/60 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Utility: Spikes / Rapid Spin / Explosion / Hidden Power Bug or Toxic**

### Why It's Good
The only Pokemon that both sets and removes Spikes. Steel/Bug typing gives many resistances. Explosion for high damage on exit. Critical team support.

### Counters
4x weak to Fire: Charizard, Fire Blast from anything. Gengar blocks Rapid Spin. Magneton.

---

name: #214 Heracross [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #214 Heracross [OU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / Sleep Absorber  
**Stats:** 80/125/75/85/40/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **RestTalk: Megahorn / Earthquake or Rock Slide / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **Swords Dance: Megahorn / Earthquake / Swords Dance / Hidden Power Rock**

### Why It's Good
125 Attack with Megahorn is devastating. Bug/Fighting hits many types super effectively. RestTalk variant absorbs Sleep Powder from Exeggutor.

### Counters
Skarmory walls completely. Gengar immune to Fighting. Flying-types. Starmie outspeeds with Psychic.

---

name: #134 Vaporeon [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #134 Vaporeon [OU]

**Role:** Bulky Water / Growth Sweeper  
**Stats:** 130/65/60/65/110/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Growth: Growth / Surf / Ice Beam / Rest**
- **Support: Surf / Ice Beam / Toxic / Rest or Haze**

### Why It's Good
Massive HP and good SpAtk. Growth boosts make it dangerous. Ice Beam hits Grass and Dragon types. Can absorb Water attacks.

### Counters
Zapdos, Raikou (Electric attacks). Snorlax. Blissey. Grass-types like Exeggutor.

---

name: #065 Alakazam [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #065 Alakazam [OU]

**Role:** Special Sweeper  
**Stats:** 55/50/45/120/135/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Psychic / Thunder Punch / Fire Punch / Recover**
- **Encore: Psychic / Thunder Punch / Encore / Recover**

### Why It's Good
120 Speed and 135 SpAtk make it one of the fastest and hardest-hitting special attackers. Elemental punches give excellent coverage.

### Counters
Snorlax, Blissey (huge SpDef). Tyranitar (Dark immune to Psychic). Pursuit users.

---

name: #200 Misdreavus [OU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #200 Misdreavus [OU]

**Role:** Spinblocker / Perish Trapper  
**Stats:** 60/60/60/85/85/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Perish Trapper: Mean Look / Perish Song / Protect / Thunderbolt or Thunder**
- **Support: Thunderbolt / Shadow Ball / Hypnosis / Pain Split**

### Why It's Good
Key Spinblocker. Normal immunity blocks Snorlax's Body Slam. Perish Song + Mean Look traps and KOs. Can spread Hypnosis.

### Counters
Tyranitar (Pursuit), Houndoom (Dark STAB). Snorlax can switch on Perish Song.

---

name: #034 Nidoking [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #034 Nidoking [UU]

**Role:** Mixed Attacker / Coverage Monster  
**Stats:** 81/102/77/85/85/75 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **MixKing: Earthquake / Ice Beam / Thunderbolt or Thunder / Lovely Kiss**

### Why It's Good
Incredible coverage with Earthquake/Ice Beam/Thunderbolt. Lovely Kiss puts threats to sleep. Decent mixed offenses and Speed. Poison typing gives Fighting resistance.

### Counters
Suicune, Starmie (outspeed, resist). Snorlax walls special attacks. Skarmory walls physical.

---

name: #196 Espeon [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #196 Espeon [UU]

**Role:** Baton Passer / Special Attacker  
**Stats:** 65/65/60/110/130/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Baton Pass: Growth / Baton Pass / Psychic / Morning Sun**
- **Offensive: Psychic / Hidden Power Fire / Morning Sun / Reflect**

### Why It's Good
130 SpAtk and 110 Speed. Best Baton Passer in GSC with Growth. Morning Sun for recovery. Can threaten sweeps on its own.

### Counters
Tyranitar, Houndoom (Dark type). Snorlax, Blissey. Pursuit trappers. Roar/Whirlwind stops Baton Pass.

---

name: #197 Umbreon [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #197 Umbreon [UU]

**Role:** Cleric / Special Wall / Mean Look Trapper  
**Stats:** 95/65/110/65/60/130 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Mean Look: Mean Look / Baton Pass / Moonlight / Charm or Toxic**
- **Cleric: Heal Bell / Toxic / Moonlight / Pursuit or Faint Attack**

### Why It's Good
Incredible special bulk (110 Def/130 SpDef). Heal Bell support. Mean Look + Baton Pass combos well. Dark typing gives Psychic immunity.

### Counters
Machamp (Fighting STAB). Heracross. Can't deal damage. Taunt (not in Gen 2).

---

name: #208 Steelix [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #208 Steelix [UU]

**Role:** Physical Wall / Phazer  
**Stats:** 75/85/200/30/55/65 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Earthquake / Roar / Toxic / Rest or Explosion**
- **CurseSteelix: Curse / Earthquake / Explosion / Rest**

### Why It's Good
200 Defense is the highest in GSC. Excellent Steel/Ground typing resists many common attacks. Roar + Spikes racks up damage.

### Counters
Water attacks (Surf, Hydro Pump). Ground-types. Special attackers. 4x weak to nothing but 30 Speed is crippling.

---

name: #006 Charizard [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #006 Charizard [UU]

**Role:** Mixed Attacker / Belly Drummer  
**Stats:** 78/84/78/100/109/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Belly Drum: Belly Drum / Fire Blast / Earthquake / Rock Slide**
- **Mixed: Fire Blast / Earthquake / Hidden Power Grass / Sunny Day**

### Why It's Good
Belly Drum is high-risk high-reward. Fire/Flying coverage. 100 Speed and decent mixed stats. Sunny Day powers up Fire Blast. Can sweep entire teams with Belly Drum.

### Counters
Blissey, Snorlax tank special hits. Starmie outspeeds. Stealth Rock (not in Gen 2). Rock Slide from faster threats.

---

name: #241 Miltank [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #241 Miltank [UU]

**Role:** Cleric / Physical Tank  
**Stats:** 95/80/105/100/40/70 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CurseTank: Curse / Body Slam / Earthquake / Milk Drink or Heal Bell**

### Why It's Good
100 Speed before Curse is excellent. Heal Bell cures team status. Milk Drink for reliable recovery. Body Slam + Earthquake covers most threats.

### Counters
Skarmory. Ghost-types immune to Body Slam + Earthquake. Machamp, Heracross.

---

name: #124 Jynx [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #124 Jynx [UU]

**Role:** Sleep Lead / Special Attacker  
**Stats:** 65/50/35/95/115/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Lead: Lovely Kiss / Ice Beam / Psychic / Dream Eater or Hidden Power Fire**

### Why It's Good
Lovely Kiss is the best sleep move (75% accuracy). Ice Beam + Psychic is great dual STAB. 95 Speed is decent. Always opens with sleep.

### Counters
Extremely frail physically (35 Def). Snorlax tanks hits. Steel-types resist both STABs. Pursuit.

---

name: #212 Scizor [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #212 Scizor [UU]

**Role:** Swords Dancer / Baton Passer  
**Stats:** 70/130/100/65/55/80 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Swords Dance: Swords Dance / Steel Wing / Hidden Power Bug / Baton Pass**

### Why It's Good
130 Attack with Swords Dance. Steel/Bug typing has many resistances. Baton Pass passes SD boosts. Only weak to Fire.

### Counters
4x weak to Fire. Skarmory. Zapdos. Any Fire move KOs.

---

name: #150 Mewtwo [Uber]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #150 Mewtwo [Uber]

**Role:** Special Sweeper / Stallbreaker  
**Stats:** 106/110/90/130/154/90 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Psychic / Ice Beam or Shadow Ball / Flamethrower or Thunder / Recover**
- **CurseTwo: Curse / Psychic / Shadow Ball / Recover**

### Why It's Good
The most powerful Pokemon in Gen 2. 154 SpAtk and 130 Speed are unmatched. Huge movepool covers everything. Can go physical with Curse. Banned from standard play.

### Counters
Blissey, Snorlax (huge SpDef). Dark-types (immune to Psychic). Mewtwo mirror.

---

name: #249 Lugia [Uber]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #249 Lugia [Uber]

**Role:** Bulky Support / CurseLugia  
**Stats:** 106/90/130/110/90/154 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CurseLugia: Curse / Aeroblast / Earthquake / Recover**
- **Support: Aeroblast / Psychic / Rest / Whirlwind**

### Why It's Good
Insane 154 SpDef and 130 Def make it nearly unkillable. Curse + Aeroblast + Earthquake has excellent coverage. Recover for longevity. The ultimate tank.

### Counters
Tyranitar (Rock + Dark), Raikou (Electric), Zapdos. Roar/Whirlwind to phaze Curse boosts.

---

name: #250 Ho-Oh [Uber]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #250 Ho-Oh [Uber]

**Role:** Mixed Attacker / Sacred Fire  
**Stats:** 106/130/90/90/110/154 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Sacred Fire / Earthquake / Recover / Hidden Power Rock or Curse**

### Why It's Good
Sacred Fire has 50% burn rate, crippling physical attackers. 130 Attack and 110 SpAtk. 154 SpDef tanks special hits. Incredible power and bulk.

### Counters
Tyranitar (Rock resists Fire, STAB Rock Slide). Blissey. Water-types. Rock Slide from anything fast.

---

name: #251 Celebi [Uber]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #251 Celebi [Uber]

**Role:** Support / Heal Bell / Leech Seed Stall
**Stats:** 100/100/100/100/100/100 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Heal Bell: Heal Bell / Leech Seed / Psychic / Recover**
- **Offensive: Psychic / Giga Drain / Hidden Power Fire / Recover**
- **Baton Pass: Swords Dance / Baton Pass / Psychic / Recover**

### Why It's Good
Perfect 100 across all stats gives excellent all-around bulk and Speed. Heal Bell cures entire team. Leech Seed + Recover makes it almost unkillable. Grass/Psychic gives great offensive options. Extremely versatile.

### Counters
Snorlax (huge SpDef, Body Slam). Tyranitar (Dark immune to Psychic, Pursuit). Ho-Oh (resists Grass, Sacred Fire). Bug-types for 4x weakness.

---

name: #151 Mew [Uber]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #151 Mew [Uber]

**Role:** Versatile Support / Swords Dance Sweeper
**Stats:** 100/100/100/100/100/100 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Swords Dance: Swords Dance / Earthquake / Shadow Ball / Explosion or Rock Slide**
- **Support: Softboiled / Thunder Wave / Psychic / Explosion**
- **Special: Psychic / Ice Beam / Thunderbolt / Softboiled**

### Why It's Good
Learns almost every move in the game. Can run any set imaginable. Perfect 100 stats mean it's always adequate. Swords Dance + Earthquake + Shadow Ball has near-perfect coverage. Explosion provides a devastating exit.

### Counters
Tyranitar (Dark immune to Psychic, Pursuit). Snorlax walls special sets. Skarmory walls physical sets without Fire moves. Mewtwo outpaces.

---

name: #149 Dragonite [BL]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #149 Dragonite [BL]

**Role:** Mixed Attacker / Hazer / RestTalker
**Stats:** 91/134/95/80/100/100 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Mixed: Ice Beam / Thunderbolt / Dynamic Punch / Rest or Haze**
- **RestTalk: Thunder / Ice Beam / Rest / Sleep Talk**
- **Hazer: Haze / Thunder / Ice Beam / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Excellent mixed attacking stats (134 Atk, 100 SpAtk). Inner Focus prevents flinching. Ice Beam + Thunderbolt is great BoltBeam coverage. Haze stops setup sweepers. Good bulk at 91/95/100.

### Counters
4x weak to Ice. Snorlax tanks everything. Blissey. Faster Ice Beam users (Starmie, Cloyster). Steels resist BoltBeam.

---

name: #135 Jolteon [BL]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #135 Jolteon [BL]

**Role:** Special Attacker / Baton Passer
**Stats:** 65/65/60/130/110/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Offensive: Thunderbolt / Hidden Power Ice or Water / Baton Pass / Growth or Thunder Wave**
- **Baton Pass: Growth / Baton Pass / Thunderbolt / Hidden Power Water**

### Why It's Good
130 Speed is the fastest non-Uber in GSC. 110 SpAtk hits hard. Growth + Baton Pass can set up teammates. Thunder Wave cripples switch-ins.

### Counters
Snorlax, Blissey (huge SpDef). Ground-types immune to Thunderbolt. Raikou matches up well. Very frail physically.

---

name: #073 Tentacruel [BL]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #073 Tentacruel [BL]

**Role:** Rapid Spinner / Special Tank
**Stats:** 80/70/65/100/80/120 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Rapid Spin: Rapid Spin / Surf / Sludge Bomb / Substitute or Mirror Coat**
- **Swords Dance: Swords Dance / Sludge Bomb / Hydro Pump / Hidden Power Ground**

### Why It's Good
120 SpDef makes it a great special sponge. 100 Speed is solid. Rapid Spin clears Spikes. Poison typing absorbs Toxic Spikes (future-proof). Mirror Coat punishes special attackers.

### Counters
Starmie outspeeds with Psychic. Earthquake users. Snorlax. Physical attackers exploit weak Defense.

---

name: #233 Porygon2 [BL]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #233 Porygon2 [BL]

**Role:** Special Tank / CursePorygon
**Stats:** 85/80/90/60/105/95 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CursePorygon2: Curse / Return / Ice Beam / Recover**
- **Special: Ice Beam / Thunderbolt / Thunder Wave / Recover**

### Why It's Good
Excellent mixed bulk (90 Def/95 SpDef). Curse + Return becomes very dangerous. Recover keeps it healthy. Good special coverage with BoltBeam. Normal typing has only one weakness.

### Counters
Machamp, Heracross (Fighting STAB). Skarmory walls Curse sets. Gengar immune to Return. Misdreavus.

---

name: #235 Smeargle [BL]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #235 Smeargle [BL]

**Role:** Spore Lead / Baton Passer
**Stats:** 55/20/35/75/20/45 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Spore Lead: Spore / Spikes / Agility or Belly Drum / Baton Pass**
- **Full Support: Spore / Encore / Destiny Bond / Agility**

### Why It's Good
Spore is the best sleep move (100% accuracy). Can learn EVERY move via Sketch. Baton Pass + Belly Drum/Agility passes massive boosts. Spikes + Spore disrupts immediately. Despite terrible stats, its movepool makes it uniquely threatening.

### Counters
Extremely frail — any hit KOs. Insomnia Pokemon (not in Gen 2). Faster Pokemon prevent Spore. Lum Berry (not in Gen 2). Roar/Whirlwind stops Baton Pass chains.

---

name: #115 Kangaskhan [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #115 Kangaskhan [UU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / RestTalker
**Stats:** 105/95/80/90/40/80 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **RestTalk: Rest / Sleep Talk / Return / Earthquake**
- **Curse: Curse / Return / Earthquake / Rest**
- **Shadow Ball: Return / Earthquake / Shadow Ball / Rest**

### Why It's Good
105 HP and decent 95 Atk. 90 Speed is solid for UU. Shadow Ball catches Ghosts trying to switch in on Return. RestTalk is very reliable.

### Counters
Skarmory, Steelix wall completely. Machamp, Heracross (Fighting). Ghost-types (unless Shadow Ball).

---

name: #125 Electabuzz [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #125 Electabuzz [UU]

**Role:** Special Attacker / Mixed Attacker
**Stats:** 65/83/57/105/95/85 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Mixed: Thunderbolt / Ice Punch / Cross Chop / Thunder Wave or Pursuit**
- **Special: Thunderbolt / Ice Punch / Hidden Power Water / Thunder Wave**

### Why It's Good
105 Speed and 95 SpAtk. Cross Chop gives unique mixed coverage hitting Snorlax and Blissey on their weak side. Ice Punch + Thunderbolt BoltBeam combo.

### Counters
Ground-types immune to Thunderbolt. Snorlax still tanks. Very frail. Raikou outclasses in OU.

name: #122 Mr. Mime [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #122 Mr. Mime [UU]

**Role:** Baton Passer / Special Attacker
**Stats:** 40/45/65/90/100/120 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Baton Pass: Calm Mind (not available) / Barrier / Baton Pass / Psychic / Encore**
- **Special: Psychic / Thunderbolt / Fire Punch / Encore**

### Why It's Good
120 SpDef is excellent. Encore locks opponents into bad moves. Barrier + Baton Pass boosts teammates. 100 SpAtk with Psychic STAB hits hard.

### Counters
Extremely frail physically (40 HP, 65 Def). Tyranitar, Houndoom (Dark). Pursuit trappers. Steel-types.

name: #211 Qwilfish [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #211 Qwilfish [UU]

**Role:** Spiker / Physical Attacker
**Stats:** 65/95/75/85/55/55 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Spiker: Spikes / Sludge Bomb / Surf / Curse or Self-Destruct**
- **Swords Dance: Swords Dance / Sludge Bomb / Hydro Pump / Self-Destruct**

### Why It's Good
Only UU Pokemon that gets both Spikes and Self-Destruct. 95 Atk is decent. Water/Poison typing gives useful resistances. Swords Dance makes it threatening before exploding.

### Counters
Starmie spins Spikes, outspeeds. Ground-types for Sludge Bomb. Special attackers exploit 55 SpDef. Skarmory.

name: #195 Quagsire [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #195 Quagsire [UU]

**Role:** Physical Wall / Hazer
**Stats:** 95/85/85/35/65/65 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Wall: Earthquake / Surf / Haze / Rest**
- **CurseQuag: Curse / Earthquake / Sludge Bomb or Hidden Power Rock / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Water/Ground typing gives Electric immunity (only weakness is Grass 4x). Haze stops all setup sweepers. Curse makes it bulky. Counters Electric-types completely.

### Counters
4x Grass weakness — any Grass move KOs. Exeggutor, Victreebel. Special attackers with non-Electric moves.

name: #097 Hypno [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #097 Hypno [UU]

**Role:** Special Wall / Calm Mind Lite
**Stats:** 85/73/70/67/73/115 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Support: Psychic / Thunder Wave / Hypnosis / Rest**
- **Counter: Psychic / Seismic Toss / Thunder Wave / Counter**

### Why It's Good
115 SpDef walls most special attackers in UU. Hypnosis + Thunder Wave provides status spreading. Counter punishes physical switch-ins. Insomnia prevents sleep (only Gen 3+, not in Gen 2 — relies on bulk).

### Counters
Tyranitar, Houndoom (Dark type). Physical attackers. Snorlax outbulks. Heracross.

name: #210 Granbull [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #210 Granbull [UU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / Heal Bell
**Stats:** 90/120/75/45/60/60 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **CurseBull: Curse / Return / Earthquake / Rest or Heal Bell**
- **Physical: Return / Earthquake / Shadow Ball / Lovely Kiss**

### Why It's Good
120 Attack is massive for UU. Lovely Kiss puts threats to sleep. Curse + Return becomes devastating. Heal Bell cures team status. Normal typing with solid bulk.

### Counters
Skarmory, Steelix wall. Very slow (45 Speed). Ghost-types immune to Return unless Shadow Ball. Fighting-types.

name: #221 Piloswine [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #221 Piloswine [UU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / Ice STAB
**Stats:** 100/100/80/50/60/60 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Earthquake / Ice Beam / Rock Slide / Rest**
- **Curse: Curse / Earthquake / Ice Beam / Rest**

### Why It's Good
Ice/Ground STAB is fantastic offensive coverage. 100/100 HP/Atk is solid. Earthquake + Ice Beam hit almost everything for at least neutral damage. Unique typing in UU.

Water-types (Surf/Hydro Pump). Fighting-types. Grass-types. Very slow and specially frail.

name: #040 Wigglytuff [NU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #040 Wigglytuff [NU]

**Role:** Cleric / Special Tank
**Stats:** 140/70/45/45/85/50 (HP/Atk/Def/Spd/SpAtk/SpDef)

### Recommended Sets

- **Support: Thunder Wave / Body Slam / Counter / Rest**

### Why It's Good
140 HP gives huge effective bulk. Thunder Wave spreads paralysis. Counter punishes physical attacks against its terrible Defense. Decent NU pick.

Ghost-types immune to Body Slam + Counter. Steel-types resist. Fighting-types.

name: #062 Poliwrath [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #062 Poliwrath [UU]

**Role:** Belly Drummer / Bulky Water

Belly Drum at +6 Attack with 95 base is terrifying. Lovely Kiss puts a counter to sleep. Water/Fighting typing is solid defensively. 90/95/90 bulk is respectable.

name: #076 Golem [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #076 Golem [UU]

**Role:** Physical Attacker / Exploder

### Recommended Sets

- **Standard: Earthquake / Rock Slide / Explosion / Curse or Hidden Power Bug**

### Why It's Good
120 Atk and 130 Def with Explosion makes it a devastating physical presence. Rock/Ground STAB has great coverage. Explosion is one of the strongest in the game.

name: #171 Lanturn [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #171 Lanturn [UU]

**Role:** Special Tank / Electric Immunity Lure

name: #089 Muk [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #089 Muk [UU]

name: #127 Pinsir [UU]
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] #127 Pinsir [UU]

**Role:** Swords Dance Sweeper

name: Design Origins & Name Etymology
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Design Origins & Name Etymology

### Starters
- **Cyndaquil** — "Cinder" + "quill" (fire hedgehog). Based on the echidna.
- **Totodile** — "Tot" (small) + "crocodile". Baby crocodile design.
- **Chikorita** — "Chicory" (plant) + "-ita" (small, Spanish). Sauropod dinosaur with a leaf.

### Legendary Beasts
- **Raikou** — "Rai" (thunder, Japanese) + "kou" (emperor). Saber-toothed tiger with thunderclouds.
- **Entei** — "En" (flame) + "tei" (emperor). Lion with a volcanic mane.
- **Suicune** — "Sui" (water) + "kune" (from "kunitama," spirit of the land). Aurora borealis mane. Crystal's mascot.

### Legendary Birds
- **Ho-Oh** — "Houou" (phoenix in Japanese). Based on the Fenghuang, a Chinese/Japanese mythological bird.
- **Lugia** — Possibly from Latin "lugeo" (to mourn) or a play on "silver" (its game). Created by Takeshi Shudo for the anime movie first.

### New Type Pokemon
- **Umbreon** — "Umbra" (shadow, Latin) + "eon" (Eevee suffix). Originally intended to be Poison-type, changed to Dark during development.
- **Espeon** — "ESP" (extrasensory perception) + "eon." Cat-like psychic design.
- **Scizor** — "Scissors" + possibly "razor." Red coloring from Metal Coat evolution.
- **Steelix** — "Steel" + "onyx." The original Steel/Ground type.

### Notable Others
- **Wobbuffet** — "That's the way it is!" (Japanese: Sonnansu). Based on a punching bag toy.
- **Smeargle** — "Smear" + "beagle." Painter dog. Sketch copies any move permanently.
- **Unown** — "Unknown." 26 forms representing the Latin alphabet.
- **Shuckle** — "Shuck" (shell) + "turtle." Highest Defense and SpDef base stats possible (230 each) when combined with Power Trick (future gen).
- **Dunsparce** — "Dun" + "dunce" + tsuchinoko (Japanese snake cryptid). One of the most underwhelming Pokemon by design.
- **Delibird** — "Delivery" + "bird." Only move: Present (random damage or healing).
- **Tyrogue** — "Tyro" (beginner) + "rogue." Baby form of the Hitmon family.

---

name: Unique Quirks and Mechanics
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Unique Quirks and Mechanics

### Ditto + Transform
- Ditto transforms into the opposing Pokemon, copying its stats, moves, and type
- In Gen 2, Transform copies the target's DVs (IVs), which affects Shiny status
- Ditto breeding: Ditto can breed with ANY Pokemon that can breed (except other Ditto)
- Ditto is the universal breeding partner — essential for breeding rare males

### Smeargle + Sketch
- Sketch permanently copies the last move used by the opponent
- Can learn ANY move in the game (except Struggle)
- Sketch has 1 PP — use it, then level up to get a new Sketch
- Smeargle can learn Spore (100% accuracy sleep), which is otherwise Paras/Parasect exclusive
- Competitive Smeargle runs Spore + Spikes + Baton Pass (terrifying support set)

### Wobbuffet's Unique Moveset
- Learns only 4 moves ever: Counter, Mirror Coat, Safeguard, Destiny Bond
- Cannot attack directly — only retaliates
- Shadow Tag (ability) traps opponents... but abilities don't exist in Gen 2
- In Gen 2, Wobbuffet is actually not very good — it can't trap opponents

### Unown's Hidden Power
- Unown only knows Hidden Power (can never learn anything else)
- Hidden Power type and base power are determined by DVs
- Different Unown letters have different DV combinations, so different Hidden Power types
- Unown is extremely weak competitively — 336 BST with only one move

### Magikarp's Splash
- Splash does literally nothing (no effect, ever)
- Magikarp can also learn Tackle (Lv15) and Flail (Lv25) in Gen 2
- Despite the memes, Magikarp is not the weakest Pokemon — Sunkern has lower BST (180 vs 200)

### Sunkern — The Weakest Pokemon
- 180 BST — lowest of all 251 Pokemon
- 30 across all stats
- Evolves into Sunflora with Sun Stone (which is... still not very good)

---

name: Shiny Pokemon
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Shiny Pokemon

### Mechanics
- Shiny status determined by DVs (IVs)
- Shiny DVs: Attack DV must be 2, 3, 6, 7, 10, 11, 14, or 15. Def, Speed, and Special DVs must all be exactly 10.
- Probability: 1/8192 (0.012%)
- Shiny Pokemon have sparkle animation when entering battle
- In Gen 2, shininess is visible in the overworld (different palette)

### Guaranteed Shinies
- **Red Gyarados** at Lake of Rage (always Shiny)
- **Odd Egg** from Pokemon Communication Center has 14% Shiny chance (Crystal only)

### Shiny Breeding
- If one parent is Shiny, offspring have a 1/64 chance of being Shiny
- The Red Gyarados is useful as a Shiny breeding parent
- DVs are partially inherited, increasing Shiny odds with Shiny parents

### Shiny-Locked
No Pokemon are "Shiny-locked" in Gen 2. Every encounter (including gifts and legendaries) can be Shiny.

---

name: Hidden/Cut Content
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Hidden/Cut Content

### Beta Pokemon
- **Ruins of Alph** had an unused puzzle room layout (BetaRuinsOfAlphUnsolvedPuzzleRoom.blk)
- Cut trainer classes and dialogue found in data files
- Several unused map warps exist in the ROM

### Debug Features
- Debug menu exists in the code but is inaccessible in retail
- Can be re-enabled via hacking — provides Pokemon/item editors, warp controls
- The debug build had a "walk through walls" flag

### Spaceworld 1997 Demo
- Early beta (leaked 2018) had drastically different Pokemon designs
- Many Gen 2 Pokemon had completely different sprites and typing
- Baby Pokemon like "Kurstraw" (baby Jynx), "Pudi" (baby Growlithe) were cut
- An entire evolution line for Ditto was planned and cut
- Farfetch'd was supposed to have an evolution

### Unused Items
- GS Ball had no event trigger in non-Japanese Crystal until Virtual Console
- Several key items have placeholder descriptions in the code
- The Teru-sama items are leftover debugging placeholder items

---

name: Version Differences (Gold vs Silver vs Crystal)
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Version Differences (Gold vs Silver vs Crystal)

### Crystal-Exclusive Features
- **Animated sprites** — Front sprites animate when Pokemon enters battle
- **Suicune storyline** — Eusine character, static Suicune encounter at Tin Tower
- **Battle Tower** — Olivine City post-game facility
- **Buena's Password** — Daily radio quiz show for points/prizes
- **Move Tutors** — Flamethrower, Thunderbolt, Ice Beam (one each per save)
- **Odd Egg** — From Pokemon Communication Center
- **GS Ball event** — Celebi encounter (Virtual Console worldwide)
- **Expanded Ruins of Alph** — More events and dialogue
- **Character selection** — Can play as female character (Kris)
- **Communication Center** — Mobile System GB features (Japan only originally)

### Wild Pokemon Differences
Pokemon availability varies between versions:

| Pokemon | Gold | Silver | Crystal |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| Vulpix/Ninetales | No | Yes | Yes (Route 36/37) |
| Growlithe/Arcanine | Yes | No | No |
| Mankey/Primeape | Yes | No | No |
| Meowth/Persian | No | Yes | No |
| Spinarak/Ariados | Yes | No | Yes (night) |
| Ledyba/Ledian | No | Yes | Yes (morning) |
| Teddiursa/Ursaring | Yes | No | Yes (Route 45) |
| Phanpy/Donphan | No | Yes | Yes (Route 45, morning) |
| Gligar | Yes | No | Yes (Route 45) |
| Skarmory | No | Yes | Yes (Route 45) |
| Mantine | Yes | No | Yes (Route 41) |
| Delibird | No | Yes | Yes (Ice Path) |
| Mareep | Yes | Yes | Yes (Route 32, morning/day only in Crystal) |
| Remoraid | Yes | Yes | Swarm only (Crystal) |

### Suicune Differences
- **Gold/Silver:** Suicune roams Johto like Raikou and Entei
- **Crystal:** Suicune has a scripted storyline; encountered at specific locations before a static battle at Tin Tower

name: Interesting Battle Mechanics Trivia
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Interesting Battle Mechanics Trivia

### Sleep Mechanics
- Sleep counter is 1-7 turns (randomly chosen when put to sleep)
- In Gen 2, if you switch out a sleeping Pokemon and switch back in, the sleep counter resets — this is a bug
- Rest always puts you to sleep for exactly 2 turns (you wake up on turn 3)

### Critical Hit Quirks
- Gen 2 crit rate is based on Speed stat: base rate = Speed/512
- Focus Energy DOUBLES the crit rate (it was bugged to divide in Gen 1)
- Scope Lens adds +1 stage to crit rate
- Moves like Slash, Crunch, Cross Chop have +1 crit stage

### Confusion Damage
- Confusion self-hit uses a typeless 40-power physical attack
- It uses your own Attack and Defense for damage calculation
- This means high-Attack Pokemon hurt themselves MORE when confused
- DynamicPunch always confuses but only has 50% accuracy

### Type Effectiveness Oddity
- Ghost is super effective against Psychic in Gen 2 (unlike Gen 1 bug where it was ineffective)
- But the only Ghost-type attacking move with decent power is Shadow Ball (80 power, Special)
- Lick (30 power) and Night Shade (fixed damage) are the only other Ghost attacks

### The Dragon Fang Bug
- Dragon Fang is supposed to boost Dragon-type moves by 10%
- Due to a bug, it actually doesn't boost Dragon-type moves at all
- It boosts... nothing. The item is completely useless in Gen 2.

---

name: Famous Players and Strategies
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Famous Players and Strategies

### Top Pokemon by Usage (GSC OU)
1. Snorlax (~90% usage) — On virtually every competitive team
2. Raikou (~35%) — Premier Electric type
3. Zapdos (~30%) — Alternative Electric
4. Skarmory (~25%) — Physical wall + Spikes
5. Starmie (~25%) — Rapid Spin + offense

### Iconic Strategies
- **SkarmBliss** — Skarmory (physical wall) + Blissey (special wall) core. Incredibly annoying to break through.
- **Spikes + Phazing** — Skarmory/Forretress set Spikes, then Roar/Whirlwind forces switches to rack up residual damage.
- **CurseLax** — Snorlax with Curse/Rest/Sleep Talk/Body Slam. The most feared sweeper in GSC.
- **Perish Trapping** — Misdreavus/Gengar with Mean Look + Perish Song. Trap and KO in 3 turns.

### Speed Tiers (Key Thresholds)
| Speed | Pokemon |
|-------|---------|
| 130 | Jolteon, Aerodactyl |
| 120 | Alakazam |
| 115 | Starmie, Raikou |
| 110 | Espeon, Gengar |
| 100 | Zapdos, Charizard, Miltank, Tentacruel, Dragonite (approximate) |
| 95 | Heracross |
| 90 | Machamp, Kangaskhan |
| 85 | Nidoking, Scizor |
| 80 | Dragonite, Marowak |
| 70 | Skarmory, Steelix |

name: Easter Eggs
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Easter Eggs

### Red at Mt. Silver
- The final boss is Red (the protagonist of Gen 1)
- His team: Pikachu Lv81, Espeon Lv73, Snorlax Lv75, Venusaur Lv77, Charizard Lv77, Blastoise Lv77
- Defeating Red rolls credits. The game continues afterward.
- Red does not speak — his only dialogue is "..."

### Professor Oak's Pokemon Talk
- Professor Oak's radio show gives out (mostly useless) Pokemon advice
- Can trigger "Pokemon Music" which plays special remixed music from Gen 1

### Buena's Password (Crystal)
- Daily radio show at 6 PM - midnight
- Listen for the password, then go to the Radio Tower to claim points
- Points exchange for rare items: Rare Candy, Nugget, HP Up, etc.

### Hidden Rocket Admin Imposter
- During the Radio Tower takeover, a Rocket Admin disguises himself as the Radio Tower Director
- If you talk to him in the underground, he reveals himself and battles you

### Trainer House (Viridian City)
- Daily battle against a trainer whose team mirrors the last person you linked/Mystery Gifted with
- If you haven't linked, you fight a default trainer named CAL with:
  Meganium Lv50, Typhlosion Lv50, Feraligatr Lv50

### Mom's Savings
- Your mom saves money from your winnings (if you let her)
- She occasionally buys items for you: Super Potion, Repel, etc.
- She can also buy decorations for your room
- Bug: She sometimes buys useless items at full price

name: Legendary Pokemon
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Legendary Pokemon

### Suicune (Crystal Storyline)

**Location:** Multiple encounters, final battle at Tin Tower
**Level:** 40
**Mechanics:** In Pokemon Crystal (unlike Gold/Silver), Suicune has a unique storyline. You encounter it at:
1. Burned Tower (flees, alongside Raikou and Entei)
2. Cianwood City (cutscene, flees)
3. Route 42 (cutscene, flees)
4. Vermilion City (cutscene, flees)
5. Route 14 (cutscene, flees)
6. **Tin Tower B1F** (static battle, Lv40) — requires Clear Bell + all 3 Legendary Beasts seen

**Catch Rate:** 3 (hardest tier)
**Moves:** Rain Dance, Gust, Aurora Beam, Mist
**Held Item:** None

### Raikou (Roaming)

**Location:** Roaming Johto (after Burned Tower event)
**Level:** 40
**Mechanics:** Appears randomly in Johto grass areas. Flees immediately on encounter. Track via Pokedex after first sighting. Its HP and status persist between encounters.
- Changes route every time you change routes
- Cannot encounter it on water or in caves
- If KOed, it is gone forever (no respawn)

**Catch Rate:** 3
**Moves:** Roar, Thunder Shock, Reflect, Crunch
**Tips:** Use Mean Look/Spider Web to trap, or use a Master Ball. False Swipe + Sleep is optimal.

### Entei (Roaming)

**Location:** Roaming Johto (after Burned Tower event)
**Level:** 40
**Mechanics:** Same roaming mechanics as Raikou.

**Catch Rate:** 3
**Moves:** Roar, Fire Spin, Stomp, Flamethrower
**Tips:** Same strategy as Raikou. Roar will force you out, so use a faster Pokemon with Mean Look.

### Lugia

**Location:** Whirl Islands B2F
**Level:** 60
**Requirements:** Silver Wing (from Radio Tower Director after Team Rocket event)
**Catch Rate:** 3
**Moves:** Aeroblast, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

### Ho-Oh

**Location:** Tin Tower 7F
**Level:** 60
**Requirements:** Rainbow Wing (from Radio Tower Director after Team Rocket event, Crystal only) + Clear Bell
**Catch Rate:** 3
**Moves:** Sacred Fire, Safeguard, Gust, Recover

### Celebi (GS Ball Event)

**Location:** Ilex Forest Shrine
**Level:** 30
**Requirements:** GS Ball (originally Japan-only mobile event; available on Virtual Console worldwide)
**Mechanics:** Take GS Ball to Kurt in Azalea Town. After one day, he returns it. Place it on the shrine in Ilex Forest. Celebi appears.
**Catch Rate:** 45 (much easier than other legendaries)
**Moves:** Leech Seed, Recover, Heal Bell, Safeguard

---

name: Roaming Mechanics (Raikou & Entei)
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Roaming Mechanics (Raikou & Entei)

- Both are released when you fall through the Burned Tower floor
- They roam Johto routes (grass encounters only)
- Each time you enter a new route, they move to a random adjacent route
- If you enter their current route, there's a chance to encounter them in grass
- They always attempt to flee on the first turn (Roar forces you out too)
- HP, status, and catch attempts persist across encounters
- If you KO either one, they disappear permanently
- Using Fly/Teleport to a route they're on works — they don't move when you Fly
- The Pokedex map feature shows their current location after first sighting

name: Gift Pokemon
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Gift Pokemon

### Starter Pokemon
**Location:** New Bark Town (Professor Elm's Lab)
**Level:** 5
**Choose one:**
- **Cyndaquil** — Fire type
- **Totodile** — Water type
- **Chikorita** — Grass type

### Togepi Egg
**Location:** Violet City Pokemon Center
**Given by:** Mystery Egg from Mr. Pokemon (Route 30), hatched by Professor Elm's aide
**Level:** 5 (when hatched)
**Egg moves vary:** Togepi hatches knowing Growl, Charm, and 2 random moves from parents (but this is the scripted egg)

### Eevee
**Location:** Goldenrod City (Bill's house, after meeting him in Ecruteak)
**Level:** 20
**Note:** Only one per save file. Choose evolution wisely.

### Shuckle (Mania)
**Location:** Cianwood City (Kirk/Mania's house)
**Level:** 15
**Held Item:** Berry
**Note:** He asks you to take care of it. He may ask for it back later — say no to keep it.

### Spearow (Kenya)
**Location:** Route 35 gate
**Given by:** Guard in the gate
**Level:** 10
**Held Item:** Mail (letter to deliver to Route 31)
**OT:** Webster
**Note:** Deliver the mail to the guy on Route 31, or keep Kenya. If you remove the mail, you can keep the Spearow but can't deliver the letter.

### Tyrogue
**Location:** Mt. Mortar (deep interior)
**Given by:** Blackbelt Kiyo (after defeating him)
**Level:** 10
**Note:** Evolves at Level 20 based on Atk vs Def stats: Hitmonlee (Atk > Def), Hitmonchan (Atk < Def), Hitmontop (Atk = Def)

### Dratini
**Location:** Dragon's Den (after answering Elder's quiz correctly)
**Level:** 15
**Special:** If you answer ALL questions correctly, the Dratini knows ExtremeSpeed (the only way to get ExtremeSpeed Dratini in the game)
**Note:** The "correct" answers are the ones about caring for Pokemon, not power

### Red Gyarados
**Location:** Lake of Rage
**Level:** 30
**Type:** Water/Flying
**Note:** Always Shiny (guaranteed). Static encounter — battle or flee, but it's unique. Gives Red Scale when caught (trade to Mr. Pokemon for Exp. Share).

### Sudowoodo
**Location:** Route 36 (blocking the path)
**Level:** 20
**Requirements:** SquirtBottle from Goldenrod flower shop (need Whitney badge)
**Note:** One per save file. Use SquirtBottle to make it attack.

### Snorlax
**Location:** Route 11 (Kanto, blocking Diglett's Cave entrance)
**Level:** 50
**Requirements:** PokeFlute channel on the radio (from Lavender Tower upgrade)
**Note:** One per save file. Tune the radio to the PokeFlute channel while standing next to it.

### Odd Egg (Crystal Exclusive)
**Location:** Pokemon Communication Center (replaces Goldenrod Pokemon Center 2F in Crystal)
**Level:** 5 (when hatched)
**Contains one of:** Pichu, Cleffa, Igglybuff, Tyrogue, Smoochum, Elekid, Magby
**Special:** 14% chance of being Shiny (compared to normal 1/8192)

name: Overview
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Overview

- **Location:** Olivine City (Crystal only, does not exist in Gold/Silver)
- **Availability:** After entering the Hall of Fame (beating the Elite Four)
- **Format:** Single battles, 3v3 (bring 3 Pokemon)
- **Goal:** Win 7 consecutive battles to complete a streak

---

name: Rules and Restrictions
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Rules and Restrictions

### Team Rules
- You must register exactly 3 Pokemon
- All 3 Pokemon must be different species (no duplicates)
- All 3 Pokemon must hold different items (no duplicate held items)
- Pokemon are NOT healed between battles (items used are consumed)

### Level Groups
The Battle Tower scales to your Pokemon's levels. When you register, your highest-level Pokemon determines the level group:

| Level Group | Your Max Level | Opponent Level |
|-------------|---------------|----------------|
| Level 10 | 1-10 | 10 |
| Level 20 | 11-20 | 20 |
| Level 30 | 21-30 | 30 |
| Level 40 | 31-40 | 40 |
| Level 50 | 41-50 | 50 |
| Level 60 | 51-60 | 60 |
| Level 70 | 61-70 | 70 |
| Level 80 | 71-80 | 80 |
| Level 90 | 81-90 | 90 |
| Level 100 | 91-100 | 100 |

### Banned Pokemon (Ubers Check)
The following Pokemon cannot be entered:
- Mewtwo
- Mew
- Lugia
- Ho-Oh
- Celebi

### Other Restrictions
- Eggs cannot be entered
- You can save and quit mid-streak (resume later)
- If you lose, the streak resets to 0

---

name: Opponent Generation
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Opponent Generation

### Trainer Pool

The Battle Tower uses a pool of pre-built trainers with preset names and classes.

**Crystal 1.0 Trainers (21 trainers):**

| # | Class | Name |
|---|-------|------|
| 1 | Fisher | HANSON |
| 2 | Pokemaniac | SAWYER |
| 3 | Guitarist | MASUDA |
| 4 | Scientist | NICKEL |
| 5 | Pokefan M | OLSON |
| 6 | Lass | ZABOROWSKI |
| 7 | Youngster | WRIGHT |
| 8 | Hiker | ALEXANDER |
| 9 | Teacher | KAWAKAMI |
| 10 | Pokefan M | BICKETT |
| 11 | Kimono Girl | SAITO |
| 12 | Boarder | CRAWFORD |
| 13 | Picnicker | DIAZ |
| 14 | Biker | ERICKSON |
| 15 | Juggler | FAIRFIELD |
| 16 | Pokefan F | HUNTER |
| 17 | Firebreather | HILL |
| 18 | Swimmer F | JAVIER |
| 19 | Swimmer M | KAUFMAN |
| 20 | Skier | LANCASTER |
| 21 | Camper | McMAHILL |

**Crystal 1.1 Additional Trainers (49 more, for 70 total):**
OBRIEN, FROST, MORSE, YUFUNE, RAJAN, RODRIGUEZ, SANTIAGO, STOCK, THURMAN, VALENTINO, WAGNER, YATES, ANDREWS, BAHN, MORI, BUCKMAN, COBB, HUGHES, ARITA, EASTON, FREEMAN, GIESE, HATCHER, JACKSON, KAHN, LEONG, MARINO, NEWMAN, NGUYEN, OGDEN, PARK, RAINE, SELLS, ROCKWELL, THORNTON, TURNER, VAN DYKE, WALKER, MEYER, JOHNSON, ADAMS, SMITH, TAJIRI, BAKER, COLLINS, SMART, DYKSTRA, EATON, WONG

**Easter Eggs:** MASUDA (Junichi Masuda, Pokemon composer), TAJIRI (Satoshi Tajiri, Pokemon creator)

### Pokemon Pool

Each level group has 21 unique pre-built Pokemon (BATTLETOWER_NUM_UNIQUE_MON = 21). Opponents are assigned 3 random Pokemon from this pool.

**Sample Pokemon (Level 10 Group):**

| Pokemon | Held Item | Moves |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Jolteon | MiracleBerry | Thunderbolt, Hyper Beam, Shadow Ball, Roar |
| Espeon | Leftovers | Mud-Slap, Psychic, Psych Up, Toxic |
| Umbreon | Gold Berry | Shadow Ball, Iron Tail, Psych Up, Toxic |

All Pokemon have preset stat experience, DVs, moves, and held items. They are fully competitive builds appropriate for their level group.

### Trainer Class Bug (Crystal 1.0)

In Crystal 1.0, there is a bug where the trainer class is not read correctly. Instead, the game uses the 6th character of the trainer's name to determine their sprite/class for dialogue purposes. This was fixed in Crystal 1.1.

---

name: Battle Mechanics
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Battle Mechanics

### Party Size
- BATTLETOWER_PARTY_LENGTH = 3
- Both player and opponent use exactly 3 Pokemon

### Streak Length
- BATTLETOWER_STREAK_LENGTH = 7
- Win 7 consecutive battles to complete a streak

### Challenge States
The game tracks your challenge state:
1. **BATTLETOWER_NO_CHALLENGE** — Not in a challenge
2. **BATTLETOWER_SAVED_AND_LEFT** — Mid-challenge, saved and exited
3. **BATTLETOWER_CHALLENGE_IN_PROGRESS** — Currently battling
4. **BATTLETOWER_WON_CHALLENGE** — Completed 7 wins
5. **BATTLETOWER_RECEIVED_REWARD** — Collected the prize

### AI Behavior
- Battle Tower opponents use standard trainer AI (no special enhanced AI)
- They use held items effectively (Leftovers for recovery, berries for status cure)
- They have competitive movesets with good type coverage
- No switching AI — they fight with whatever is sent out

---

name: Rewards
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Rewards

After winning 7 consecutive battles, you receive a reward. The reward selection is handled by BATTLETOWERACTION_CHOOSEREWARD and BATTLETOWERACTION_GIVEREWARD.

Possible rewards include:
- **Vitamins:** HP Up, Protein, Iron, Calcium, Carbos
- **Rare items:** Rare Candy
- The specific reward varies by streak number

---

name: Strategy Tips
text:
[Pokemon Crystal] Strategy Tips

### Team Building
- Bring 3 Pokemon that cover each other's weaknesses
- Leftovers is the best held item (sustain over 7 battles without healing)
- Recovery moves (Rest, Recover, Milk Drink) are invaluable since you don't heal between battles
- Avoid self-KO moves (Explosion, Self-Destruct) — you need Pokemon alive for 7 battles

### Recommended Pokemon
- **Snorlax** — CurseLax with Rest/Sleep Talk/Body Slam/Curse. Nearly unkillable.
- **Starmie** — Fast, Recover, good coverage with Surf/Thunderbolt/Psychic/Recover
- **Suicune** — Incredibly bulky, RestTalk with Surf/Ice Beam
- **Blissey** — Walls all special attackers, Softboiled for healing, Toxic for damage

### Level Group Strategy
- Level 100 group has the toughest opponents with maxed stats
- Lower level groups can be "gamed" by bringing a strong Lv10 Pokemon
- Level 10 group is surprisingly competitive — opponents have good moves even at low levels
